title: failed to run rootfull container when trying to mount device block
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Running rootfull container when trying to mount block device results in error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a loop device
```
[root@fedora cosmin]# ls -al /dev | grep ^b
brw-rw----   1 root   disk    253,     0 Jun  1 22:15 dm-0
brw-rw----   1 root   disk      7,     1 Jun  1 22:29 loop1
brw-rw----   1 root   disk    259,     0 Jun  1 22:15 nvme0n1
brw-rw----   1 root   disk    259,     1 Jun  1 22:15 nvme0n1p1
brw-rw----   1 root   disk    259,     2 Jun  1 22:15 nvme0n1p2
brw-rw----   1 root   disk    259,     3 Jun  1 22:15 nvme0n1p3
brw-rw----   1 root   disk      8,     0 Jun  1 22:15 sda
brw-rw----   1 root   disk      8,     1 Jun  1 22:15 sda1
brw-rw----   1 root   disk    252,     0 Jun  1 22:15 zram0
```
2.  Execute:
```
podman run -it --rm --privileged --mount=type=bind,src=/dev/loop1,dst=/mnt --log-level=trace --security-opt label=disable fedora:35 /bin/bash
```


**Describe the results you received:**
```

comments.body:
Can you try the `runc` runtime instead, to see if this is a crun issue?

@giuseppe PTAL

Using `runc` I got this:
```
DEBU[0000] Container b7a0fd837e66a1fe4d2a614c944b02cc002ce2ac32a2eacd061788b6c64007ec storage is already unmounted, skipping...

If you mount -v /mnt:/mnt does it work?

So this is something to do with a block device being added to a container.

Could you try to mount the block device on a subdir of /mnt?

--device /dev/loop1:/mnt/loop1

If that works, then it might be something about mounting a device on a directory?  If not, then maybe we have difficulty creating block devices.

@giuseppe @AkihiroSuda Should runtimes fail if user attempts to mount a device on a  directory inode?

Hello,
After some digging in `runc`, I've found that the mount is done by a syscall:
https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/blob/a7a45d7d2721314ea325874c2adc69723f6b7162/libcontainer/mount_linux.go#L57

Now, I tried to achieve the same thing with this code:
```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
)

func main() {
	var flags int
	flags |= unix.MS_BIND | unix.MS_RDONLY
	err := unix.Mount("/dev/loop1", "/mnt", "bind", uintptr(flags), "")

	fmt.Println(err)
}
```
which gives the same error. If I'm changing `bind` by `ext4` it works but in podman there is no such type `ext4`.
So, I guess this is the right behavior. We cannot mount block devices with `mount` option.

Am I missing something here?
no, you are right.  There is no way to mount directly a block device.  You'd need to create the mount first, then bind mount it in the container
Ok. Thanks a lot for your help guys. I'm closing the issue if it is ok for you.
Should we catch this error in Podman and have a better error message?
